moduleDescription: Perform the operation return the new value, all memory models are validPerform the operation return the old value, all memory models are valid

entries.name: AtomType
entries.code: AtomType = SomeNumber | pointer | ptr | char | bool
entries.description: Type Class representing valid types for use with atomic procs

entries.name: AtomMemModel
entries.code: AtomMemModel = distinct cint

entries.name: ATOMIC_RELAXED
entries.code: ATOMIC_RELAXED {.importc: "__ATOMIC_RELAXED", nodecl.}: AtomMemModel
entries.description: No barriers or synchronization.

entries.name: ATOMIC_CONSUME
entries.code: ATOMIC_CONSUME {.importc: "__ATOMIC_CONSUME", nodecl.}: AtomMemModel
entries.description: Data dependency only for both barrier and synchronization with another thread.

entries.name: ATOMIC_ACQUIRE
entries.code: ATOMIC_ACQUIRE {.importc: "__ATOMIC_ACQUIRE", nodecl.}: AtomMemModel
entries.description: Barrier to hoisting of code and synchronizes with release (or stronger) semantic stores from another thread.

entries.name: ATOMIC_RELEASE
entries.code: ATOMIC_RELEASE {.importc: "__ATOMIC_RELEASE", nodecl.}: AtomMemModel
entries.description: Barrier to sinking of code and synchronizes with acquire (or stronger) semantic loads from another thread.

entries.name: ATOMIC_ACQ_REL
entries.code: ATOMIC_ACQ_REL {.importc: "__ATOMIC_ACQ_REL", nodecl.}: AtomMemModel
entries.description: Full barrier in both directions and synchronizes with acquire loads and release stores in another thread.

entries.name: ATOMIC_SEQ_CST
entries.code: ATOMIC_SEQ_CST {.importc: "__ATOMIC_SEQ_CST", nodecl.}: AtomMemModel
entries.description: Full barrier in both directions and synchronizes with acquire loads and release stores in all threads.

entries.name: atomicLoadN
entries.code:
proc atomicLoadN[T: AtomType](p: ptr T; mem: AtomMemModel): T {.
    importc: "__atomic_load_n", nodecl, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: This proc implements an atomic load operation. It returns the contents at p. ATOMIC_RELAXED, ATOMIC_SEQ_CST, ATOMIC_ACQUIRE, ATOMIC_CONSUME.

entries.name: atomicLoad
entries.code:
proc atomicLoad[T: AtomType](p, ret: ptr T; mem: AtomMemModel) {.
    importc: "__atomic_load", nodecl, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: This is the generic version of an atomic load. It returns the contents at p in ret.

entries.name: atomicStoreN
entries.code:
proc atomicStoreN[T: AtomType](p: ptr T; val: T; mem: AtomMemModel) {.
    importc: "__atomic_store_n", nodecl, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: This proc implements an atomic store operation. It writes val at p. ATOMIC_RELAXED, ATOMIC_SEQ_CST, and ATOMIC_RELEASE.

entries.name: atomicStore
entries.code:
proc atomicStore[T: AtomType](p, val: ptr T; mem: AtomMemModel) {.
    importc: "__atomic_store", nodecl, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: This is the generic version of an atomic store. It stores the value of val at p

entries.name: atomicExchangeN
entries.code:
proc atomicExchangeN[T: AtomType](p: ptr T; val: T; mem: AtomMemModel): T {.
    importc: "__atomic_exchange_n", nodecl, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: This proc implements an atomic exchange operation. It writes val at p, and returns the previous contents at p. ATOMIC_RELAXED, ATOMIC_SEQ_CST, ATOMIC_ACQUIRE, ATOMIC_RELEASE, ATOMIC_ACQ_REL

entries.name: atomicExchange
entries.code:
proc atomicExchange[T: AtomType](p, val, ret: ptr T; mem: AtomMemModel) {.
    importc: "__atomic_exchange", nodecl, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: This is the generic version of an atomic exchange. It stores the contents at val at p. The original value at p is copied into ret.

entries.name: atomicCompareExchangeN
entries.description: This proc implements an atomic compare and exchange operation. This compares the contents at p with the contents at expected and if equal, writes desired at p. If they are not equal, the current contents at p is written into expected. Weak is true for weak compare_exchange, and false for the strong variation. Many targets only offer the strong variation and ignore the parameter. When in doubt, use the strong variation. True is returned if desired is written at p and the execution is considered to conform to the memory model specified by success_memmodel. There are no restrictions on what memory model can be used here. False is returned otherwise, and the execution is considered to conform to failure_memmodel. This memory model cannot be __ATOMIC_RELEASE nor __ATOMIC_ACQ_REL. It also cannot be a stronger model than that specified by success_memmodel.

entries.name: atomicCompareExchange
entries.description: This proc implements the generic version of atomic_compare_exchange. The proc is virtually identical to atomic_compare_exchange_n, except the desired value is also a pointer.

proc atomicAddFetch[T: AtomType](p: ptr T; val: T; mem: AtomMemModel): T {.
    importc: "__atomic_add_fetch", nodecl, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

proc atomicFetchAdd[T: AtomType](p: ptr T; val: T; mem: AtomMemModel): T {.

proc atomicFetchOr[T: AtomType](p: ptr T; val: T; mem: AtomMemModel): T {.

proc atomicFetchAnd[T: AtomType](p: ptr T; val: T; mem: AtomMemModel): T {.

proc atomicFetchXor[T: AtomType](p: ptr T; val: T; mem: AtomMemModel): T {.

entries.code:
proc atomicFetchNand[T: AtomType](p: ptr T; val: T; mem: AtomMemModel): T {.

entries.name: atomicTestAndSet
entries.code:
proc atomicTestAndSet(p: pointer; mem: AtomMemModel): bool {.
    importc: "__atomic_test_and_set", nodecl, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: This built-in function performs an atomic test-and-set operation on the byte at p. The byte is set to some implementation defined nonzero "set" value and the return value is true if and only if the previous contents were "set". All memory models are valid.

entries.name: atomicClear
entries.code:
proc atomicClear(p: pointer; mem: AtomMemModel) {.importc: "__atomic_clear",
    nodecl, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: This built-in function performs an atomic clear operation at p. After the operation, at p contains 0. ATOMIC_RELAXED, ATOMIC_SEQ_CST, ATOMIC_RELEASE

entries.name: atomicThreadFence
entries.code:
proc atomicThreadFence(mem: AtomMemModel) {.importc: "__atomic_thread_fence",
    nodecl, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: This built-in function acts as a synchronization fence between threads based on the specified memory model. All memory orders are valid.

entries.name: atomicSignalFence
entries.code:
proc atomicSignalFence(mem: AtomMemModel) {.importc: "__atomic_signal_fence",
    nodecl, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: This built-in function acts as a synchronization fence between a thread and signal handlers based in the same thread. All memory orders are valid.

entries.name: atomicAlwaysLockFree
entries.description: This built-in function returns true if objects of size bytes always generate lock free atomic instructions for the target architecture. size must resolve to a compile-time constant and the result also resolves to a compile-time constant. ptr is an optional pointer to the object that may be used to determine alignment. A value of 0 indicates typical alignment should be used. The compiler may also ignore this parameter.

entries.name: atomicIsLockFree
entries.code:
proc atomicIsLockFree(size: int; p: pointer): bool {.
    importc: "__atomic_is_lock_free", nodecl, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: This built-in function returns true if objects of size bytes always generate lock free atomic instructions for the target architecture. If it is not known to be lock free a call is made to a runtime routine named __atomic_is_lock_free. ptr is an optional pointer to the object that may be used to determine alignment. A value of 0 indicates typical alignment should be used. The compiler may also ignore this parameter.

entries.name: fence
entries.code: template fence()

entries.name: atomicInc
entries.code:
proc atomicInc(memLoc: var int; x: int = 1): int {.inline, discardable,
    raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Atomically increments the integer by some x. It returns the new value.

entries.name: atomicDec
entries.code:
proc atomicDec(memLoc: var int; x: int = 1): int {.inline, discardable,
    raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Atomically decrements the integer by some x. It returns the new value.

entries.name: cas
entries.code: proc cas[T: bool | int | ptr](p: ptr T; oldValue, newValue: T): bool

entries.name: cpuRelax
entries.code: proc cpuRelax() {.inline, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
